Blood Runed Short Sword  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 18
DMG: 9 Dmg Bonus: 35
Backstab DMG: 9
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Lifespike (Combat, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 5.0 Size: TINY
Class: WAR SHD BRD ROG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

i am 53 barbarian warrior, only about 125 sta, 5 weight doesn't matter at all, mine dosem't move at all ... i don't use it for proc, the proc sucks ... but 9 / 18 .. and i got it for free , how can you say no ? plus you gotta be atleast 51 + to loot one, failed experiments is lvl 58and kicked my groups ***, but we took him the second time, i won roll, it was cool .

__DEL__1595780191797 wrote:
i am 53 barbarian warrior, only about 125 sta, 5 weight doesn't matter at all, mine dosem't move at all ... i don't use it for proc, the proc sucks ... but 9 / 18 .. and i got it for free , how can you say no ? plus you gotta be atleast 51 + to loot one, failed experiments is lvl 58and kicked my groups ***, but we took him the second time, i won roll, it was cool .



reflecting on this persons comment, this is somewhat untrue.

this is one of the "easier" weapons to obtain for a level 1 proccing weapon, which is awesome.

the other is crystalline spider fang.

▲ Page top
Not Bad
QuoteReply
# Apr 30 2001 at 2:42 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I got one last night in Velk's and so far I like it. I use it as my primary and a Lam as my secondary. I have noticed no significant change in my stamina dropping and the thing is fast as hell. The only problem is a warriors pierce skill caps lower than his 1hs skill, but not by a huge amount. Its a worthwhile weapon until I cane get into Sleepers Tomb or VP and get some truely uber weapons.

Callabran Steelshark
56 Warrior
Seventh Ward
Drinal Server

P.S. The thing procs about 4 to 6 times a fight with a dex of 122. No big help but sure does not hurt.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 21 2001 at 9:45 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) its a teink weapon for sure. the weaight is not a big factor for the lower battels. Nornally there is not that much clearing of high hp mobs and 11 lifetap would make a nce little add to the hp of a lvl20 or below

whats that proc do? it sounds kewl, but knowing verant, since this weapon has uber stats the proc probly isnt much.
▲ Page top
RE: proc?
QuoteReply
# Dec 21 2000 at 7:42 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The proc is an 11 dmg lifetap.
▲ Page top
RE: proc?
QuoteReply
# Dec 21 2000 at 3:42 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592245759462
Scholar
308 posts
Lifespike is the level 4 necromancer lifetap. Shadow Knights also get this lifetap spell at level 15, although it has an increased mana cost due to the hybrid casting penalty. At maximum efficiency, it costs 18 mana and yields 11 point lifetap. Even though this lifetap is very small, in the hands of a Shadow Knight using Vampiric Embrace it would largely increase the amount of health stolen. In the hands of any other melee character, a lifetap proc is still a pretty good thing: lower target's health, increase your own health.

As an added bonus, this weapon has a pretty low delay, so you can swing it faster in a given timeframe, which increases the opportunities for the weapon to proc. Also, this proc is a lifetap, and the lifetap line of spells work on a very different resistance table, so this proc will seldom be resisted.
▲ Page top
RE: proc?
QuoteReply
# Dec 21 2000 at 2:06 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591749817370
Scholar
397 posts
I'm sorry but an 11 dmg lifetap on something that weighs 5 kinda and swings every 18 isnt that great.

I have an iksar monk using a trance stick (iksars have very high dex) and he maybe procs 2-4 times on a blue mob in a fight. This means a warrior would get back like 80hps in a fight if he had high dexterity. In my opinion it's not worth having such a heavy weapon with such a low delay that would cause your stamina to drop this much. If I were a melee class I would stick to lighter weapons if you are going to use some that are very fast. Still it's not too bad, just not great. I would use this if you cannot get an appropriate weapon and this thing drops when you raid to velk.
▲ Page top
RE: proc?
QuoteReply
# Mar 30 2001 at 7:55 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
5.0 isn't so bad, when I used an Iksar Beserker Club in my offhand the sta didn't drop so bad and it was 8.1...and that guy who said his sta dropped pretty quick with lammys musta had pretty low sta cuz mine doesn't more than a yellow bub even after those long multiple mob battles with da lammy and a sword with a 2.1 weight on it, it's not a weapon that I'd actively seek out only to get it but if I got it I probably wouldn't try to hard to upgrade it...any extra hps coming in are good hps, who knows might even look bad ****
▲ Page top
RE: proc?
QuoteReply
# Jan 04 2001 at 11:09 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
My warrior has 88 dex, and I still get 2-6 procs in EVERY fight with a blue or higher. The longer the fight the more procs, just dropped Tse in the frozen tower, she resisted 7 procs. To a warrior with 140+ str(which most of us do)that 5 weight is nothing, so what if our sta drops like a rock, so we slow down...oh wait,look at the speed delay...well gosh it evens out,wow! There are far better weapons, if you don't want it don't get it. If you do have it, sell it to a newbie or a ebay'er for 12k. I'm done with my rant, sorry for stepping on anyone's toes.
▲ Page top
RE: proc?
QuoteReply
# Jan 15 2001 at 4:07 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It's no drop guy, you can't sell it. Although why would you want to? I can get a good 8 - 12 proc's with 140dex. get me a lute of the howler, or have an enchanter cast illusion werewolf on me, and i'll have life tap along with lifespike going on. also with 200dex, i'll be procin like mad.
